title: Terebinthus schaffneri
intro: Terebinthus schaffneri, also known as Guajolote tree, is a species of evergreen tree native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Burseraceae family. The Guajolote tree has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Terebinthus schaffneri include sesquiterpenes, diterpenes, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the Guajolote tree is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and resin, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
